positions.selection: No - Oklahoma City wins by over 7.5 points
positions.settlement_note: OKC won by 9, so OKC did win by over 7.5. The NO side lost.

model_read.pregame_leak: The spread position underestimated OKC's turnover-pressure floor and late offensive rebounding buffer. The model saw SAS money backing the spread and Game 1 rebounding dominance, but did not penalize SAS enough for ball-security risk without Fox and Castle primary-handler volatility.
model_read.liveflow_success: The halftime SAS team-total over was correctly identified because the Spurs had 51 points despite 13 turnovers, strong offensive rebounding, perfect first-half FT shooting, and a clear second-half efficiency recovery path. They scored 62 in the second half.
model_read.primary_failure_mode: Cover-side confusion plus pressure-adjusted margin miss: SAS offensive profile was strong enough for team total but not clean enough to stay inside +7.5 due to turnover bleed and OKC possession leverage.
